items.prompt: In reproducible research, continuous integration primarily:
choices: Automatically tests and validates changes to code or data pipelines
Replaces the need for any licenses or consent
Guarantees FAIR Findable without identifiers or metadata
Only formats slides for the lecture website
items.explain: Section 13.8.1 defines CI as automatically testing and validating pipeline changes.

items.prompt: CI checks on preprocessing and inputs can catch broken prerequisites before downstream analysis runs.
items.explain: Example 13.37 uses CI to validate preprocessing and required inputs.

items.prompt: Cross-environment consistency tests are meant to detect:
choices: Dependency or environment mismatches that change results across machines
Only typography differences in PDF exports
Only lecture clip bridge text length
items.explain: Example 13.38 focuses on tests that keep results consistent across environments.

items.prompt: Why should CI retest model training after code or data updates?
items.rubric: Accept catching regressions, metric drops, training failures, or keeping published training path aligned with the repo.
items.sample_answer: To catch training failures or metric regressions before publishing updated results.

items.prompt: Automated workflow documentation helps keep the published narrative synchronized with what the pipeline actually ran.
items.explain: Section 13.8 discusses automating workflow documentation so records stay aligned with execution.
